seoTitle: How to Change an Epic Color in Jira
metaDescription: Change the color tag on a Jira epic so it's easier to visually distinguish from other epics on your board.
intro: When a project has several epics running at once, their color tags are often the fastest way to tell them apart on a board. This guide covers how to change an epic's color in Jira.

steps.heading: Open the epic
steps.body: Navigate to the epic you want to update and open its detail view.

steps.heading: Find the epic color field
steps.body: Locate the color swatch or epic details section where the current color is displayed.

steps.heading: Choose a new color
steps.body: Click the color field and select a new one from the available palette.

steps.heading: Save the change
steps.body: Confirm the update so the new color is applied to the epic.

steps.heading: Check the board for the update
steps.body: Go back to your board and verify the epic's tag now reflects the new color everywhere it appears.

faq.question: Does changing an epic's color affect its linked issues?
faq.answer: No, the color only changes how the epic label appears visually; it doesn't affect the issues linked to it.

faq.question: Can I choose a custom color outside the default palette?
faq.answer: Jira typically limits epic colors to a preset palette rather than allowing fully custom color codes.

faq.question: Where does the epic color show up besides the board?
faq.answer: The epic color also appears in the backlog view and on any card or panel that displays the epic label.

faq.question: Who can change an epic's color?
faq.answer: Anyone with edit permissions on the epic can typically update its color, not just project administrators.
